title: Underwater Pathtracer Effect
summary: Modify a starter C++ pathtracer that renders a plain Cornell-box spheres scene so its canonical render becomes an underwater image matching a target reference.
taskPrompt:
You are working on a Linux VM. Your task is to modify a C++ pathtracer so a plain Cornell-box spheres render becomes an underwater scene matching the provided target reference.

## Task Directory
- `base`

## Visible Inputs
- Baseline render with no underwater effect: `base/input/baseline_no_underwater.png`
- Target visual reference: `base/input/target_reference.png`
- Detailed task brief: `base/input/task_prompt.md`

## Starter Code
- C++ pathtracer source: `base/software/pathtracer`
- Render wrapper: `base/software/pathtracer/render.sh`

## Your Task
1. Inspect the baseline image and target reference.
2. Modify the C++ renderer and/or scene assets under `base/software/pathtracer`.
3. Implement a convincing underwater effect: reflective/refractive water surface, blue-green water attenuation, visible water volume/fog, waterline, and plausible light interaction through the surface.
4. Run `base/software/pathtracer/render.sh` to build and preview your result.
5. Write the final artifacts under `base/output`:
   - `final.png`
   - `run_manifest.json`

## Constraints
- Do not modify files under `base/input`.
- Work only from the visible `input/` images and the provided `software/pathtracer` source tree.
- The evaluator will rebuild and rerun your modified renderer with its own canonical CMake + pathtracer command. A hand-edited or copied PNG is not sufficient.
- Keep final deliverables inside `base/output`.
agentMustDo: Inspect the public baseline and target reference PNGs.
Modify the C++ pathtracer source or scene assets under software/pathtracer.
Implement visible underwater rendering effects including waterline/surface, blue-green attenuation, fog or participating-medium appearance, and reflective/refractive light interaction.
Run software/pathtracer/render.sh so the renderer writes output/final.png.
Write output/final.png and output/run_manifest.json under output/.

inputFiles.name: baseline_no_underwater.png
inputFiles.description: Starter renderer output before underwater effects are implemented.

inputFiles.name: target_reference.png
inputFiles.description: Public visual target for the underwater effect.

inputFiles.name: task_prompt.md
inputFiles.description: Agent-visible task brief and output contract.

evaluation: The evaluator rebuilds and reruns the modified renderer from source, then visually assesses whether output/final.png is a coherent underwater version of the provided Cornell-box scene.

evaluatorCredentials.purpose: OpenAI vision/LLM judge scores the deliverable during evaluate(); sourced from secret/eval_time/openai.env

classifier.rationale: The task involves modifying a pathtracer to create a visual effect, which aligns with 3D animation and interactive media.
